title: 2-deoxy-D-glucose chemical exchange sensitive spin-lock (CESL) MRI of the rodent brain
author: Philipp Boehm-Sturm

steps_raw.instruction: Goal: injected dose of 1 g/kg body weight.

The MRI protocol includes the following scan protocols (Boehm-Sturm, et al., 2025). Glossary: RARE: rapid acquisition with relaxation enhancement (turbo spin echo sequence) WASABI: Simultaneous mapping of water shift and B1 (Schuenke P, Windschuh J, et al., 2017) T1rho: CESL sequence to map R1rho=1/T1rho DWI: diffusion weighted imaging FAIR: flow sensitive alternating inversion recovery (sequence for perfusion imaging) EPI: echo planar imaging (DWI and FAIR use spin echo EPI readout)
01_1_Localizer When the animal is placed into the scanner first tune and match (wobble) the volume resonator. Run scan with Automatic Setup / Adjustments.
01_2_Localizer_multi_slice Run scan.
02_T2_TurboRARE_mapshim

Set Geometry a. Reduce the number of slices to 6, adjust the slice position so that the anterior commissure is covered by the uppermost slice. b. Switch back to 38 slices and adjust center to trachea. c. Adjust the ellipsoid shim volume to the center of the brain. d. Make sure that the read offset is 0.00. a. slice positioning b. /c. final geometry and shim volume position d. check read offset

03_WASABI_B0_B1_mapping Import the slice orientation of 02_T2_TurboRARE_mapshim and add 2.625 mm to the slice offset! B0 and B1 mapping is recommended to check B0/B1 homogeneity in the given experimental setup and to correct for inhomogeneities in the image post processing step. In our experimental setting, the B0 and B1 maps were only used once to check sufficient homogeneity and not used for corrections. WASABI is particularly efficient in mapping B0 and B1 but other sequences can be used. The slice offset is specific to the MCAO model to ensure the center of the striatum is covered, which is the brain region most severely affected by the stroke. In other disease models different offsets can be used to perform 2DG-CESL MRI in the brain regions of interest. a. set the slice offset b. final geometry to cover the center of the striatum Run scan.
Import the slice orientation of 03_WASABI_B0_B1_mapping to all following T1rho protocols (04_T1rho_pre to 06_T1rho_post)
05_6_T1rho_post2DG to 05_35_T1rho_post2DG Run scans.
06_T1rho_post2DG_lowres Run scan.

07_DWI_Trace_EPI_sat Diffusion (DWI) and perfusion (PWI) MRI are of specific interest in the stroke model. These sequences can also be of interest in other disease contexts but are not mandatory for CESL imaging. Import the slice orientation of 02_T2_TurboRARE_mapshim and adjust the FOV Saturation slice. final geometry with FOV saturation slice Run scan.

08_Perfusion_FAIR_EPI_studyshim a. Import the slice orientation of 03_WASABI scan. b. Use the study shim option. Use of a global shim ensures a better global inversion pulse of the FAIR sequence. a. final geometry b. check study shim option Run scan.

Animals undergo two surgical procedures before the MRI examination: a. Femoralis catheter implantation to inject 2DG during the MRI measurements. b. 90 min middle cerebral artery occlusion (MCAO) to induce focal cerebral ischemia. All animal procedures mentioned herein are specific to stroke modeling in the rat using 90 min MCAO and can be replaced with other disease models.

Anesthetize animals using isoflurane in a 70%/30% N2O/O2 mixture. Anesthesia is induced with 5% isoflurane and maintained at 1.75-2.5% isoflurane during the femoralis catheter placement and the MCAO and 1.75% during MRI to achieve a breathing rate of ~60-100/min.
For MRI, place the animal on a heated water blanket to ensure constant body temperature of 37±0.5 °C.
Use ear and toothbar fixation to minimize motion artifacts.

BioSpec (70/20 USR, Bruker, https://www.bruker.com/ ) Bruker 7 Tesla system (BioSpec 70/20 USR, Bruker, Ettlingen, Germany) + 86 mm transmit volume resonator (Bruker) + receive rat brain surface coil (Bruker). Paravision 6.0.1 software.
Monitor the respiration rate using a small animal monitoring system (SA Instruments, Stony Brook, NY, USA).
B0 map Create a B0 map with: a. 3 averages, default FOV and b. Map S/N 20. a. B0 map settings FOV and averages b. B0 map setting Reconstruction Run B0 mapping (Start).
⇒ Slow injection of 2DG solution through the femoralis catheter over 2 min! Inject the calculated volume plus the dead volume of the catheter.
04_T1rho_pre2DG_lowres 05_1_T1rho_pre2DG to 05_5_Tirho_pre2DG Run scans.
Preparation of the injection solution: 25% 2DG w/v, i.e. 0.25 g in 1 mL of solution. ~3.3 mL NaCl and 1 g 2DG were needed to achieve 4 mL of the final solution.
Injection volume: v = dose * weight / concentration = 1 g/kg * weight / 0.25g / mL = 4 mL/kg. Example: 1.6 mL for a rat of 400 g body weight.
Injection period: around 1-2 min.

Region of Interest (ROI) Analysis ROI analysis can be performed in two ways: ROI analyses. CESL images can be quantified in anatomical regions defined in the SIGMA rat brain atlas (a) or using manual delineation (b) of the lesion on ADC, CBF and late ΔR 1ρ maps (mean of the last 5 maps). Penumbra is defined via perfusion/diffusion mismatch. Figure reproduced from Boehm-Sturm et al., 2025 (doi: 10.1177/0271678X251355049 ) under a CC-BY-NC license.

Automatic analysis of brain regions of interest Segment brain regions automatically on the different MRI contrasts (T2, ADC, CBF, CESL) using a series of 3D to 3D and 3D to 2D image registrations using the SIGMA rat brain template (Barriere, et al., 2019). The registration can be performed for example in ANTx2 software (Koch, et al., 2019). ANTx2 all all https://github.com/ChariteExpMri/antx2 ChariteExpMRI Apply the registration to the SIGMA atlas, which matches the template. For registration, use the b = 0 image for diffusion MR images, the first inversion time image for perfusion MR images and the first TSL image for CESL images. For perfusion MRI, images will be distorted due to shimming artefacts of the EPI sequence and a nonlinear registration is needed. For ADC and CESL images, affine (12 degrees of freedom) registrations are sufficient.

Manual analysis of stroke regions of interest This step is only executed for the stroke model. It could be of interest for other animal models to segment special regions of interest manually but is not mandatory. Segment the hypointense lesion on ADC, CBF and ΔR 1ρ maps (mean image of the last 5 images) using an initial automated histogram-based segmentation followed by manual corrections in ANALYZE software (v5.0, AnalyzeDirect, Overland Park, KS, USA). Using the image registration transforms of the first approach, transform the lesion masks from ADC and CBF to the atlas and then register them to the ΔR 1ρ maps. In atlas space, generate ROIs mirrored at the midline and transform them to the ΔR 1ρ maps. Define lesion core via hypointense ADC, hypoperfused tissue via hypointense CBF and the hypometabolic lesion via hypointense areas on the mean ΔR 1ρ maps (mean of the last 5 measurements). The penumbra is defined via perfusion diffusion mismatch, i.e.
Reagent: 2-Desoxy-D-Glucose Carl Roth Catalog #CN96.3 (CAS: 154-17-6)
Run scan 02_T2_TurboRARE_mapshim.
Statistics Extract mean values from regions of interest. Perform group statistical analyses on those values with tests matching the experimental design of your study.

Prepare phantom with 5 mm NMR tubes containing different concentrations of 2DG in buffer. Typical range of concentrations: (0, 0.02 M, 0.04 M, 0.06 M, 0.08 M, 0.10 M) 2DG in DPBS: Gibco DPBS without Calcium and Magnesium Fisher Scientific Catalog #14190136 or equivalent

Perform the in vivo CESL MRI protocol excluding the DWI and PWI scans. A single T1rho scan is sufficient. Use a single slice perpendicular to the NMR tubes (cf. T1rho protocol in section "In vivo MRI").
Generate R 1ρ map ( cf. section "Image data processing").

Plot R 1ρ over 2DG concentration and perform a linear fit. The result should be similar to this CESL MRI of tubes containing different concentrations of 2DG in DPBS. R 1ρ map (a) and quantification including linear fit (b). Figure reproduced from Boehm-Sturm et al., 2025 (doi: 10.1177/0271678X251355049 ) under a CC-BY-NC license.
Generate R 1ρ maps Calculate R 1ρ (t i ) at time t i voxelwise via monexponential fitting of the signal over the spin lock times TSL:
